system_description: Japanese case particle disambiguation. Postpositional case particles (が ga subject, を wo object, に ni indirect object/direction/time, で de location/means, から kara source, まで made goal) unambiguously mark argument roles regardless of word order. Topic marker は wa can replace が ga or を wo, triggering topic-comment reanalysis. The は/が distinction is the primary ambiguity site: は wa marks topic (what the sentence is about), が ga marks exhaustive focus subject (who specifically). This distinction is resolved by information structure, not syntax.
notes: Japanese case particles are post-nominal and immediately follow each argument, compressing role ambiguity as each phrase is parsed. The dominant rule weight (0.56) is high because particles are unambiguous for core grammatical roles. The は/が distinction is the genuine ambiguity site and the most linguistically interesting DCP falsifier: both can mark subjects, but the compression event (which one fires) signals information structure.
